findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed in source: `api_secret_key` defaults to a literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share". Committing secrets in code is a critical credential-exposure risk (OWASP A02 Cryptographic Failures / A05 Security Misconfiguration): the value is now in version control history forever, exposed to anyone with repo read access, and any deployment that does not override APP_API_SECRET_KEY will silently use this well-known default — trivially exploitable for forging signatures, sessions, or tokens that depend on it.
findings.suggestion: Do not provide a literal default. Require the value from the environment, e.g. `api_secret_key: SecretStr` with no default (or `Field(...)`) so pydantic-settings raises at startup if APP_API_SECRET_KEY is unset; use `SecretStr` to avoid accidental logging. Rotate this key immediately and purge it from git history (git filter-repo / BFG) since it is now compromised.
